intro: On paper Cohere and Azure OpenAI Service look similar, but the day-to-day ergonomics diverge: Cohere scores 5/5 on developer experience against Azure OpenAI Service's 4/5. Cohere is LLM API focused on enterprise text, embeddings, and RAG. Azure OpenAI Service is Azure-hosted OpenAI models with enterprise compliance and regional endpoints. Pick Cohere when the job is api gateway and rate limiting and you accept medium lock-in; pick Azure OpenAI Service when it is api gateway and rate limiting and you accept medium lock-in. The data we have shows Cohere at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience, and Azure OpenAI Service at mostly transparent pricing and docs with solid developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Cohere is a poor fit for internal, simple projects, and Azure OpenAI Service is a poor fit for internal, simple projects. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Cohere is for LLM API focused on enterprise text; Azure OpenAI Service is for Azure-hosted OpenAI models; decide based on ecosystem fit.
verdict_a: Choose Cohere if day-to-day developer ergonomics are a priority and the work maps to LLM API focused on enterprise text, embeddings, and RAG.
verdict_b: Choose Azure OpenAI Service if some ecosystem coupling is acceptable and the work lines up with Azure-hosted OpenAI models with enterprise compliance and regional endpoints.

faq.q: Is Cohere cheaper than Azure OpenAI Service?
faq.a: Cohere starts at $4.00 on a subscription model; Azure OpenAI Service starts at $1 on a subscription model. That makes the sticker comparison depend on usage — a low entry price can get expensive once volume ramps, so price at your actual workload.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Cohere to Azure OpenAI Service?
faq.a: Migration is possible in either direction. Cohere carries medium lock-in and Azure OpenAI Service carries medium lock-in, so plan for meaningful but not blocking rework. Run both in parallel before you fully cut over.

Cohere scores higher on developer experience in our data (5/5 vs 4/5). That reflects public feedback on docs, onboarding, and day-to-day ergonomics. Still, run a spike with your own code — DX ratings don't capture every edge.

faq.q: Is Azure OpenAI Service a good alternative to Cohere?
faq.a: Yes — Azure OpenAI Service is a reasonable alternative to Cohere for api gateway and rate limiting. The practical differences are subscription-vs-subscription billing and medium-vs-medium lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Azure OpenAI Service as a credible swap.
